Config.Title: Trespasser Configuration
Config.ShowInitiativeInChatHint: If enabled, initiative rolls will be posted to the chat log.
Config.RestrictMovementActionHint: If enabled, movement is only allowed after taking a Move or Vault action. If disabled, tokens can move freely.
Config.RestrictHUDActionsHint: If enabled, HUD actions (Defend, Help, etc.) can only be used once per turn unless otherwise specified.
Config.RestrictAPFocusUsage: Restrict AP and Focus action usage
Config.RestrictAPFocusUsageHint: If enabled, the system enforces AP and Focus costs. If disabled, actions can be used without sufficient points.
Config.GroupCheckFullParty: Group Check always use full party
Config.GroupCheckFullPartyHint: If enabled, group checks will always include all party members without showing a selection popup.
Config.RestrictHavenEditToLeaderHint: Only the party leader can add or remove buildings and projects in Haven.
Config.ClockSizeHint: The width and height of all clocks in the system (Projects, Events, and Injuries).
Config.FontSizeBaseHint: The base font size for the entire system. All other font sizes will scale proportionally.
Config.BypassHavenBuildingLimitsHint: If enabled, Havens will not be restricted by the Building Limit or Build Slots defined in the progression table.

Panic.Title: Panic Level: {panic}
Panic.PanicStatus: PANIC! Peril {peril} <= Panic Level {panic}. Enemies may retreat or surrender!
Panic.DeedUsage: You can use {heavy} Heavy Deed(s) and {mighty} Mighty Deed(s) this round.

Rest.RDHowMany: How many Recovery Dice to spend?
Rest.RDRecoverNote: (Recovers max value of Skill Die per RD)
Rest.RDDialogNote: (Rolls Skill Die per RD to recover HP)
Rest.ChoiceTwiceNote: Choosing the same item twice reduces it twice.
Rest.NightRestHP: Night's Rest: HP fully recovered. RD set to current Endurance ({endurance}).
Rest.ResetCosts: Reset all Deed and Talent bonus costs and uses.
Rest.WeekRestAll: Week's Rest: All resources fully recovered.

Notifications.DropDeedsOnly: Only Deeds can be dropped here.

Notifications.DropArmorOnly: Only Effects and States can be applied to Armor.
Notifications.DropDeedsOnlyEffects: Only Effects and States can be applied to Deeds.
Notifications.DropTalentsOnly: Only Effects and States can be applied to Talents.
Notifications.DropRationsOnly: Only Effects and States can be applied to Rations.
Notifications.SlotOccupied: Slot {placement} is already occupied by {name}. Unequip it first.
Notifications.HeavyArmorLimit: You can only have one Heavy armor equipped.
Notifications.TwoHandedEquip: You must unequip both hands to equip a Two-Handed weapon.
Notifications.HandsFull: Both hands are full. Unequip a weapon first.
Notifications.InventoryFull: Inventory full! You need to drop an item before you can unequip {name}.
Notifications.NoTargetsAbort: No targets selected! Roll aborted, printing basic Accuracy for reference.
Notifications.SpendRDLimited: You tried to spend {spent} RD but only have {current}. Spending {current} instead.
Notifications.InventoryCapReached: Inventory cap reached. You cannot add more unequiped items.
Notifications.NoTargetsDefault: No targets selected! Rolling against default CD 10.
Notifications.NotEnoughFocus: Not enough Focus to use {name}. (Needs {cost}, has {current})
Notifications.NotEnoughEndurance: Not enough Endurance to use {name}. (Needs {cost}, has {current})
Notifications.NoAP: You don't have enough Action Points!
Notifications.MoveActionRequired: You must take the Move action (spend AP) before moving your token.
Notifications.MovementLimitExceeded: You have exceeded your movement limit for this turn.
Notifications.NotYourPhase: It is not this token's phase!
Notifications.VaultStraightLine: Vault must be in a straight line (orthogonal or diagonal).

Chat.RummageMessage: {name} uses {action} for {cost} AP to manage their inventory.

Chat.WaitMessage: {name} uses Wait. Turn moves to the Late Phase with {ap} AP and {move} movement points reserved.
Chat.DeedSurcharge: spent {count} Focus to use a Deed after a Maneuver.

Item.CounterEffectsNote: Dragging an effect here will make it a counter to this one, subtracting intensities when applied.

Party.NamePlaceholder: The Company of the Ashen Star
Party.NoMembers: No members yet. Drag character actors here or use the picker below.
Party.DropHint: Drag a character actor here to add them to the party
Party.DropCharactersOnly: Only character actors can be added to the party.
Party.SelectAttribute: Select an attribute for the group check.
Party.SelectParticipantsHint: Choose which party members will participate in this check.
Party.Label: Party

Dungeon.Name: Dungeon Name
Dungeon.LocationPlaceholder: e.g., Beneath the Ruins of Thornwall
Dungeon.EncounterTableHint: Link a RollTable for random encounters

Actions.ExploreDesc: Move cautiously into an adjacent unexplored room. Each character makes INTELLECT | PERCEPTION, noticing one room detail on success. Interrupted by encounters or room traps.
Actions.TraverseDesc: Move to any previously explored room or the dungeon entrance. No checks required. Fleeing the dungeon triggers one final alarm check.
Actions.InteractDesc: Engage with a feature of the current room in a complex or time-consuming way. The Judge calls for a skill check or group check as needed.
Actions.SearchDesc: Linger in an explored room to investigate it further, learning each remaining undiscovered detail. No check required.
Actions.HideDesc: Wait in silent darkness. Group AGILITY | STEALTH check. Alarm falls by 1d4 if half or more succeed, or 1d8 if all succeed. Light sources must be covered (depletion check each).
Actions.VandalizeDesc: Break open a locked door, chest, or barrier. One character makes MIGHT | ATHLETICS; others can join for a group check. Alarm rises by +1 per participant.
Actions.PickLockDesc: Attempt to pick a locked door or chest. One character makes AGILITY | TINKERING while the others keep watch.
Actions.DisarmDesc: Attempt to disarm a trap. INTELLECT | TINKERING check (or INTELLECT | MAGIC for magical traps). On a shadow, the trap springs on the acting character.
Actions.ConverseDesc: Spend a few minutes trading words with a nearby creature. Automatically consumes an action if more than a minute or two is spent talking.
Actions.MomentsRestDesc: Pause for ten minutes. Each resting character must eat or lose 1 endurance. Spend recovery dice (max value), erase one focus checkmark, regain spent armor dice.
Actions.IncantDesc: Cast an incantation. The party keeps watch while one or more characters perform their magic.

Sparks.CannyDesc: You perform the task with care. You retain something expected to be lost or avoid paying a necessary cost. Banishes costly.
Sparks.QuickDesc: You accomplish the task in short order, saving precious time. Banishes slow.
Sparks.QuietDesc: You slip beneath notice. No unwanted attention is drawn. Banishes loud.
Sparks.SafeDesc: You suffer no harm doing it, even though injury seemed certain. Banishes harmful.
Sparks.StrikingDesc: Your impressive skill influences those around you, inspiring allies or earning favor. Banishes daunting.

Shadows.CostlyDesc: You lose something of material value - treasure, a tool pushed too far, supplies dropped in haste.
Shadows.SlowDesc: The task took much longer than expected. Precious time is lost. Torches flicker. Stomachs rumble.
Shadows.LoudDesc: You draw attention with a loud noise or major disruption. Those nearby now know what you're up to.
Shadows.HarmfulDesc: You hurt yourself - anything from scratches to a lingering injury depending on the task.
Shadows.DauntingDesc: Your effort was humiliating. Observers question whether you belong here. Morale falters.

Approach.AmbushDesc: Initiate combat from hiding. The opposing side skips their turns on the first round.
Approach.FightDesc: Boldly advance with no effort to disguise intentions. Combat begins normally.
Approach.GreetDesc: Emerge peacefully with lowered weapons. The other side may choose any approach, including ambush.
Approach.WarnDesc: Make your presence known and issue threats or demands, but hold action. The other side cannot ambush.
Approach.WithdrawDesc: Retreat to your last place of safety. May lose time or supplies.

Details.Title: Encounter Details
Details.Instruction: Use these tables to generate context for the encounter as needed.

Dungeon.DepletionPrompt: Make depletion checks for active light sources.

Session.EndConfirm: Are you sure you want to end this exploration session?

Room.Name: Room Name
Room.DescriptionPlaceholder: Describe the room's appearance, contents, and atmosphere...
Room.ConnectionsHint: Drag a Room item here to create a connection
Room.RoomTrapTriggerPlaceholder: e.g., Stepping on the third flagstone...
Room.NoConnections: There are no known connections from this room.
Room.AllExplored: All connected rooms have already been explored.
Room.DetailTrapsWarning: GM WARNING: There are {count} active {label} in this room!

Connection.DropHint: Drop a Room item here to connect
Connection.DropRoomsOnly: Only Room items can be dropped here.
Connection.NeedsDungeon: This room must belong to a dungeon to create connections.
Connection.SameDungeon: The dropped room must belong to the same dungeon.

Dungeon.SimultaneousActionDesc: The party attempts two or more actions at the same time. Alarm increases by +1 for each, and the Judge makes an immediate alarm check.
Dungeon.Label: Dungeon

Result.SuccessDesc: You achieve what you intended without unforeseen complications.
Result.ShadowDesc: Your success has a cost. You get what you want, but something goes wrong.
Result.FailureDesc: You fail. Progress is halted and you must find another way or face consequences.
Result.HarmfulDesc: You hurt yourself - from scratches to a persistent injury.
Result.DauntingDesc: Your effort was humbling. Onlookers question if you belong here. Morale falters.

Dungeon.DepletionRollHint: Roll each depletion die. On a 1-2, the light source is depleted.
Dungeon.WaitingForGM: Waiting for the GM to start exploration.

Warning.NoBuildSlots: You have no available building slots (Limit: {max}).
Warning.BuildingLimitReached: You have reached the maximum building limit (Limit: {max}).

Haven.WeeksRestFlavor: Each character gains the week's rest benefits.
Haven.ResolveProductionFlavor: Process all active production chains and pay wages to active hirelings.
Haven.PopulationCheckFlavor: Make a population check to see how much the haven grows or declines.
Haven.EventCheckFlavor: Check for a new haven event (roll d10 vs Skill Bonus) or advance existing threat clock.
Haven.StagnationDesc: The Haven has reached the population rank required for the next level. Rank growth is stopped until the Haven levels up, but Sparks still trigger Arrivals.
Haven.DropLeaderHint: Drop a Character here to assign as Leader
Haven.DropHirelingToChainHint: Drop a Hireling here to add to chain
Haven.DropItemHint: Drop items here to add to Haven inventory
Haven.CharactersOnly: Only Character actors can be assigned as Leader.
Haven.HirelingMustBeInHaven: Hireling must already be in the Haven's inventory to be assigned to a chain.
Haven.DidNothing: had nothing to consume or produce.
Haven.NotEnoughFunds: Haven does not have enough funds ({cost}) to pay all hirelings! Treasury: {treasury}.
Haven.InsufficientFundsError: Cannot process week: Haven does not have enough funds to cover the balance. Current treasury: {treasury}. Please reduce costs or add funds.
Haven.TransferToCharacterOnly: Only Character tokens can receive items from the Haven.

Build.DropUpgradeHint: Drop a Building here to set as upgrade path

Haven.DropFeaturesHint: Drop Features here to provide to owner upon completion
